checks.a_analysis: The converted JSON faithfully represents the source document's content. All headings, descriptions, and notes match the text present in the source PDF. There are no fabricated sections or sentences.

I verified every URL and the email address against the source PDF. All links (IRIS, USGS, Stanford, Berkeley, ScienceCourseware) and the email for Tim Martin match the source exactly. No contact information was fabricated.
The document is a single-page flyer. All sections, from the header down to the contact note and the three logos at the bottom, are present in the JSON output.
The source document contains no tables.

checks.a_analysis: Each paragraph in the output corresponds to the information blocks in the source. Key details, such as the note about purchasing seismometers and the JAVA requirement for the ScienceCourseware link, are preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content matches the source document with no meaningful differences. Punctuation and spacing variations are within acceptable limits for an OCR-based conversion.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the output JSON matches the source PDF's content without any observed duplication of text blocks, paragraphs, or sections.

The heading hierarchy is significantly degraded. First, the document title is assigned a level 2 (H2) heading instead of level 1 (H1), and no H1 exists in the document. Second, major section headers identified in the source through bold formatting and layout (e.g., 'IRIS...', 'USGS...', 'Virtual Earthquake', etc.) are converted into regular paragraphs rather than being assigned appropriate heading levels (H2 or H3). This flattens the document structure and removes semantic navigation points for screen reader users.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual headers: 'IRIS Incorporated Research Institutions for Seismology', 'USGS United States Geological Survey Hazards Program', 'Virtual Earthquake', 'QCN Quake Catcher Network', 'Northern California Earthquake Data Center - ANSS...'
checks.b_evidence_output: Elements 3, 9, 11, 14, and 16 are all labeled as "type": "paragraph" instead of "heading". Additionally, the main title in element 1 is level 2.
checks.b_evidence_location: sections[0].elements[0], sections[0].elements[2], sections[0].elements[8], sections[0].elements[10], sections[0].elements[13], sections[0].elements[15]

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no tables; therefore, no table structural corruption is possible.
The reading order in the JSON faithfully follows the top-to-bottom, logical flow of the original document, including the header information, the listed resources, and the logos at the bottom.

The source document presents resources in a format that resembles a list, but lacks explicit bullets or numbering. The pipeline has rendered these as individual paragraphs. While less semantic than a list type, this preserves the structure and meaning of the separate entries adequately.

Several URLs clearly visible and active in the source text are not correctly hyperlinked in the output JSON. While some links (like USGS and QCN) were correctly captured with 'href' properties, others were rendered as plain text within paragraphs, losing their functionality.
checks.b_evidence_output: In elements 3, 12, and 13, the corresponding URLs are present as text objects but lack the "href" property and are not wrapped in a link object.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the same fundamental visual organization as the source PDF. The information is presented in the same order: title, contact information, list of resources, and logos. While the PDF displays three logos in a horizontal row at the bottom and the HTML displays them in a vertical stack, this is an expected change for a responsive HTML layout. The omission of the secondary header text ('Earthquakes in NC workshop') is a content-level issue, but the overall visual organization and flow of the document remain faithful to the original.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; therefore, there are no tables to evaluate in the HTML output.

All meaningful visual elements (logos) from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. Specifically, the NCDENR logo, the N.C. Geological Survey seal, and the North Carolina Emergency Management triangle logo are all rendered correctly at the bottom of the page.
A visual inspection of the HTML output confirms that there is no duplicated body content. Each list item and image appears once, following the layout of the source document.
